name: Content Repurposer

parameters.content:
## Content Repurposer

### How it works

This workflow receives a field note or anonymized case win via webhook, validates that the content does not contain privileged language, sends it to Claude (Anthropic) to generate four marketing assets (LinkedIn post, newsletter blurb, Google Business Profile post, FAQ entry), runs a post-hoc keyword safety scan on all outputs, logs the delivery event to the compliance audit sheet via the Bar-Compliance Guardrail, and emails the formatted content package to your marketing inbox.

### Setup steps

- Get an Anthropic API key from console.anthropic.com and create a Header Auth credential (Header Name: x-api-key, Header Value: your key) saved as 'Anthropic API key'.
- Set FIRM_NAME, FIRM_EMAIL, FIRM_FROM_EMAIL, and GUARDRAIL_WORKFLOW_ID in n8n project Variables.
- Wire an SMTP email credential in project → Credentials tab.
- Deploy the Bar-Compliance Guardrail workflow first and set its ID in GUARDRAIL_WORKFLOW_ID.

### Customization

Change the Claude model in Build Content Prompt from claude-sonnet-4-6 to claude-haiku-4-5-20251001 to reduce cost. Add practice-area-specific prompt instructions to tailor copy style. Extend the output format by adding new asset types to the Claude prompt and parsing them in Extract & Safety-Check Content.

## Receive and validate input

Webhook intake and validation. Accepts field notes and anonymized case wins, rejects empty submissions and content containing privileged language before any AI processing runs.

## Generate content

Builds the Claude prompt with firm config and content metadata, calls the Anthropic API, and parses the four marketing assets from the response.

## Safety check

Runs an independent post-hoc keyword scan on all four generated assets. Routes flagged content to a human-review skip rather than delivering potentially non-compliant copy.

## Log and deliver

Logs the content generation event to the compliance audit sheet via the Bar-Compliance Guardrail, then emails the four formatted assets to your marketing inbox.

nodes.name: When Content Submitted
nodes.notes:
Receives a field note or case win from your team. Copy the Production URL and give it to whoever submits content — typically a paralegal, office manager, or the attorney.

Accepted input (POST JSON body):
  content — the raw field note or case win text (required, minimum 20 characters)
  content_type — 'field_note' or 'case_win' (optional, defaults to 'field_note')
  practice_area — e.g. 'family law', 'personal injury' (optional — helps Claude tailor the copy)
  firm_name — overrides the FIRM_NAME variable for this submission (optional)

Also accepts Fluent Forms webhook format — detected automatically.

IMPORTANT: Do not include client names, case numbers, opposing party names, or any privileged details in the content field. Submit only anonymised outcomes and firm process notes. For case wins, obtain written client consent before submitting — the generated assets will reference the outcome type, not the specific client.

nodes.name: Normalize and Validate Input
nodes.notes:
Normalizes input from both Fluent Forms and direct JSON webhooks, then validates it.

Validation rules:
  Minimum 20 characters of content — rejects empty or trivially short submissions
  No privileged or legal-advice language — rejects content containing 'attorney-client', 'privileged', 'legal opinion', etc. Firms must anonymise before submitting

Output: content, content_type ('field_note' or 'case_win'), practice_area, is_valid_input, validation_note, received_at.

nodes.name: If Valid Input
nodes.notes:
Proceeds only when the submitted content passes the validation check.

Yes (is_valid_input: true) → content is present and does not contain privileged language → proceed to generate copy.
No (is_valid_input: false) → reject with a labelled skip. The validation_note field contains the reason.

nodes.name: Ignore Invalid Content
nodes.notes: Ends the workflow when the submitted content is missing, too short, or contains privileged language. No content is generated. Visible in execution history with the validation_note reason in the upstream node output.

nodes.name: Build Content Prompt
nodes.notes:
Reads firm config from n8n Variables and constructs the Claude API request payload.

Variables read:
  FIRM_NAME — appears in the prompt so Claude writes copy for this specific firm
  FIRM_EMAIL — where the content package is delivered
  FIRM_FROM_EMAIL — the sender address for the delivery email

Model: claude-sonnet-4-6. Chosen for copy quality — this template replaces agency fees, so the output needs to be publish-ready. To reduce cost at the expense of quality, change 'claude-sonnet-4-6' to 'claude-haiku-4-5-20251001' here.

The prompt instructs Claude to produce marketing copy only (no legal advice), to anonymise outcomes (no client identifiers), and to attach bar-required disclaimers ('Attorney Advertising. Past results do not guarantee future outcomes.') on case-win assets.

nodes.name: Generate Marketing Content
nodes.notes:
Sends the content to Claude (Anthropic) and receives the four marketing assets as JSON.

Credential: Settings → Credentials → New → search 'Header Auth' → Name: 'Anthropic API key' → Header Name: 'x-api-key' → Header Value: your Anthropic API key from console.anthropic.com.

The anthropic-version header (2023-06-01) is required by the Anthropic API and is added automatically by this node.

Cost: claude-sonnet-4-6 at ~2,000 output tokens costs approximately $0.03 per run. A firm submitting one piece of content per week spends roughly $1.50/year on AI costs.

Rate limits: Anthropic's default limits are generous for this use case. If you see 429 errors, check your usage tier at console.anthropic.com.

nodes.name: Extract & Safety-Check Content
nodes.notes:
Parses Claude's JSON response and runs an independent post-hoc keyword safety scan on all four outputs.

Two-layer safety check:
  Layer 1 — Claude's self-report: if Claude returns is_marketing_safe: false, the workflow routes to the human-review skip immediately.
  Layer 2 — Post-hoc scan: scans all four outputs for legal-advice indicators (attorney-client, we advise, pursuant to, case law, statute, court held, etc.). Flags any asset that matches as 'REVIEW FIRST' rather than 'READY TO PUBLISH'.

is_marketing_safe is true only if both layers pass.

Also computes character and word counts for each asset and builds the formatted email body for the delivery step.

nodes.name: If Marketing Safe
nodes.notes:
Routes based on the combined safety check result.

Yes (is_marketing_safe: true) → all four outputs passed both Claude's self-check and the keyword scan → proceed to compliance log and delivery.
No (is_marketing_safe: false) → content was flagged by Claude or by the post-hoc scan → route to the human-review skip. The content submitter should review the original field note or case win and resubmit with any legal-advice language removed.

nodes.name: Skip — flagged for human review
nodes.notes: The safety check flagged the generated content — either Claude self-reported it as unsafe, or the post-hoc keyword scan found legal-sounding language in at least one asset. No email is sent. The submitter should review the original content, remove any legal-advice or privileged language, and resubmit. Check the 'Extract & Safety-Check Content' node output for which assets were flagged and why.

nodes.name: Compliance Check — Audit Log
nodes.notes:
Calls the Bar-Compliance Guardrail to log this content generation event to your compliance audit sheet.

This workflow delivers content to an internal firm email, not to a client — so opt-out checking does not apply and approved will always be true. The primary purpose of this call is the audit log entry, which records what was generated, when, and for which practice area.

The guardrail workflow ID is read from the GUARDRAIL_WORKFLOW_ID n8n Variable (Settings → Variables). Set it to the numeric ID from the Bar-Compliance Guardrail workflow URL: .../workflow/WORKFLOW_ID.

Returns: { approved, message_out, suppression_reason } plus all upstream fields.

value.message: ={{ 'Content Repurposer generated a ' + $('Extract & Safety-Check Content').item.json.content_type + ' package for ' + $('Extract & Safety-Check Content').item.json.practice_area + ' on ' + $('Extract & Safety-Check Content').item.json.received_at }}

nodes.name: If Approved to Deliver
nodes.notes:
Routes based on the compliance guardrail's decision.

Yes (approved: true) → guardrail cleared the delivery → send the content package email to the firm.
No (approved: false) → delivery suppressed by the guardrail (unexpected for an internal email — check the GUARDRAIL_WORKFLOW_ID variable and that the Bar-Compliance Guardrail workflow is active).

nodes.name: Skip — delivery suppressed
nodes.notes: The compliance guardrail suppressed the delivery. For an internal firm email this is unexpected — check that the GUARDRAIL_WORKFLOW_ID variable is set correctly and that the Bar-Compliance Guardrail workflow (NTC-33) is active and correctly configured.

nodes.name: Email Content Package to Firm
nodes.notes:
Emails the four marketing assets to your firm's marketing inbox, formatted for direct copy-paste into each channel.

The email body contains:
  • LinkedIn post with character count and READY TO PUBLISH / REVIEW FIRST flag
  • Newsletter blurb with word count and flag
  • Google Business Profile post with character count and flag
  • FAQ entry with flag
  • Before-publishing checklist (consent reminders, bar disclaimer requirements, GBP character limit)

Credential: Settings → Credentials → New → search SMTP → enter your email provider settings → save as 'Email account (SMTP)'.
  Gmail: use an App Password (Google Account → Security → App Passwords)
  Microsoft 365: use your email and password; enable SMTP AUTH in the M365 admin center
  Zoho / other: check your provider's SMTP documentation

All content values (subject, body, from address) come from upstream nodes — never from the guardrail's message_out, which is an audit log artifact.

body.content: A small business owner came to us in a panic — their commercial landlord had served an eviction notice after they missed two months of rent during a plumbing emergency that made the space unusable. We negotiated directly with the landlord, documented the habitability issues, and got the eviction withdrawn. The client kept their lease, received a two-month rent credit, and has been in business for three more years since.

description: Takes one field note or case win and generates four ready-to-publish marketing assets: LinkedIn post, newsletter blurb, Google Business Profile post, and FAQ entry. Marketing copy only — never legal content. Replaces marketing agency copy fees ($500–2,000/mo). Target keyword: repurpose law firm content.
